text: Iranian retaliatory strikes hit Dubai International Airport and targets near Abu Dhabi, killing one person and injuring seven.
Hundreds of flights were cancelled across the Middle East as multiple countries closed their airspace to commercial aviation.
Hundreds of thousands of travellers were left stranded or diverted, with airports in the Gulf region overwhelmed by the sudden chaos.
Airlines including Emirates, Etihad, and Qatar Airways suspended all operations indefinitely, warning passengers to avoid booking new flights.
Aviation experts say it could take weeks to fully restore normal flight operations across the region.
Dubai handles over ninety million passengers a year, making it one of the world's busiest airports.
The disruption highlights how military conflict in the Middle East can instantly paralyze global air travel and supply chains.
